title: Data Sharing Docs Source Metadata Tasks
content_html:
Data Sharing Docs Source Metadata Tasks

This is the tracker for implementing Data Sharing Docs Source Metadata Request.

When the work is complete, move durable architecture notes into the owning Docs Viewer and Data Sharing docs, then close out this tracker and the parent request.

baseline verification set

Run only the checks warranted by the touched slice. The final cutover should include:

  • Focused Python syntax checks for changed Docs Viewer source/read helpers, Data Sharing services, export/import services, management/API wrappers, and tests.
  • Focused pytest for source metadata helper behavior, selectable document records, docs export, docs import review context, and stale-path guards.
  • Negative-path tests proving Data Sharing does not read public flat docs indexes, public tree/recent/search payloads, manage/local generated docs indexes, or generated by-id payloads for document metadata.
  • Local Analytics/Data Sharing API smoke checks only when browser or API route behavior changes.
  • git diff --check.

Codex sandbox note: local service, browser, and temporary localhost checks will need elevated permissions even when the product code is healthy.

general steer

  • Data Sharing is a first-class canonical-data service, not a generated-data workflow.
  • In this request, generated data means artifacts produced for publication, preview, management, report, route, search, or other site-surface workflows, regardless of whether they are public, private, manage-only, local-only, or checked in.
  • Data Sharing must use canonical-equivalent docs data from scope config, docs source front matter, and docs source body content processed through shared Docs Viewer parsing/rendering code.
  • Preserve current Data Sharing behavior, UI options, package workflows, package formats, and selectable-record fields, including published.
  • Cover every configured Docs Viewer scope through shared scope-config-driven code. Do not create scope-specific modules or repeated library/analysis code blocks.
  • Do not add metadata-index.json, tooling-index.json, or another generated metadata JSON as the Data Sharing source.
  • Do not add compatibility shims or dual-read fallbacks to generated publication artifacts.
  • Source docs should be updated; generated Docs Viewer payloads should not be rebuilt for this request unless an unrelated local watcher does so.

Implementation Tasks

Work through the batch table by ID order. A deferred row is intentionally out of the implementation path and includes the reason in the action. Allowed statuses are planned, in progress, done, and deferred.

Each batch is described in its own sibling document. Task rows inside each batch use scoped IDs such as 1.1, 1.2, and 2.1.

Batch ID status title
1 done Batch 1: Contract and Dependency Audit
2 done Batch 2: Source Metadata Read API
3 done Batch 3: Data Sharing Consumer Migration
4 done Batch 4: Contract Tests and Stale-Path Guards
5 done Batch 5: Durable Docs and Closeout

closeout coverage

Batch 5 owns the template closeout duties:

  • update docs
  • cleanup confirmation
  • final verification record
  • close out this tracker and the parent request

Closeout Notes

  • Batch 2 added the Docs Viewer-owned source metadata helper API and focused tests.
  • Batch 3 migrated selectable records, docs export/package preparation, source rendering/source-text extraction, and returned-package review context to the Docs Viewer-owned source metadata helper.
  • Batch 4 added contract guard tests and removed generated docs index/by-id source declarations from the documents adapter config.
  • Batch 5 moved the final source metadata contract into durable docs and closed this request.

Verification Log

No explicit generated write command was run; the local docs watcher regenerated Studio docs payloads after source docs edits.
